data.narrative:
SatRev is a Series B-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Zwoli­ński. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 private companies at identical stage and sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M). Public comparables in Space & Aerospace (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trade at a median 45.3x price-to-sales, though this method cannot be applied without SatRev's revenue disclosure.

The peer-median approach anchors the valuation at $700M, derived from the midpoint of the four private comparables. This range spans $274M to $1.3B, reflecting meaningful dispersion in how the market values similar-stage space companies. The peer-median method remains the primary triangulation lever given data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $595M to $805M, centered on $700M. Key uncertainties include SatRev's actual revenue and margin profile relative to peers—critical for assessing whether it clusters toward the lower quartile (Sift, Hubble Network) or upper tier (Aalyria, Stoke Space). Without disclosed financials, positioning within the $274M–$1.3B peer band depends heavily on operational traction and market differentiation not captured in this quantitative framework. The narrow triangulated range ($595M–$805M) reflects reliance on a single applicable method and should be treated as indicative rather than conclusive.
